data.description:
Make the commit-trailer format + all CLI-served guides resolve for repo-less clients

## Context / Problem

Clients using the `rebar` CLI **without a rebar checkout** hit dead ends in CLI-served prose.
Today's commit `c2bfbe217` packaged the two author guides into `src/rebar/_guides/` so
`rebar explain plan|review` serves them from any install — but it left the guides' **internal
cross-references** as consumer-relative paths (`review-policy.md`, `review-kernel.md`,
`../CONTRIBUTING.md`, `plan-review-gate.md`, `plan-review-criteria-guide.md`,
`docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md`, `AGENTS.md`), which dangle for a consumer with no such tree —
the same class of dead-consumer-link bug that `62933ff62` fixed for plan-review deep-links
("client report §5"). Separately, the required `rebar-ticket: <id>` commit-trailer format
(`EXPECTED_FORMAT` in `verify_commit.py`) has **no** CLI-discoverable home at all: only
`rebar verify-commit-ticket --help` prints it, and nothing points there. The remediation was
not comprehensive.

## Approach

Two moves, both grounded in conventions already in this repo:

1. **Give the trailer a first-class CLI home.** Add a third packaged author guide,
   `src/rebar/_guides/commit-ticket-trailer.md`, registered as the `commit-trailer` topic in
   `AUTHOR_GUIDES` (`registry.py`) — served offline by `rebar explain commit-trailer` and the
   MCP `explain_guide` tool, exactly like `plan`/`review`. It quotes `EXPECTED_FORMAT` verbatim
   (drift-pinned by test) plus how-to-self-check prose (`rebar verify-commit-ticket`).

2. **Repoint every dangling reference in the packaged guides** so it resolves for a repo-less
   consumer, using the established two-way convention: sibling packaged guides →
   `rebar explain <topic>`; repo-only docs → the canonical hosted base
   `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/blob/main/…` (the same base `62933ff62`'s
   `_DEFAULT_DOCS_URL` and pyproject `[project.urls] Documentation` already use).

Then lock it with a guard test asserting no packaged `_guides/*.md` link target is a
consumer-relative path (only `https://`, intra-doc `#anchor`, or `rebar explain` are allowed) —
so the remediation stays comprehensive. Rejected: a bespoke `rebar trailer-format` subcommand
(`explain` is already the on-ramp for `plan`/`review`; a third topic needs no new wiring);
rejected shipping the whole `docs/` tree in the wheel (heavy; hosted URLs already resolve).

## Scope

- `src/rebar/_guides/commit-ticket-trailer.md` — NEW packaged guide; quotes `EXPECTED_FORMAT`
  verbatim + `rebar verify-commit-ticket` self-check prose; links only via `https://`/`rebar explain`.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` — add `"commit-trailer"` → the new file in `AUTHOR_GUIDES`.
- `src/rebar/_guides/passing-code-review.md` — repoint L5 review-policy/review-kernel + L6/L39
  CONTRIBUTING to hosted URLs; L8 sibling → `rebar explain plan`; L35 trailer →
  `rebar explain commit-trailer` + `rebar verify-commit-ticket --help`.
- `src/rebar/_guides/writing-a-passing-plan.md` — repoint L6 criteria-guide + L7
  plan-review-gate to hosted URLs; L8 sibling → `rebar explain review`.
- `tests/unit/test_verify_commit_ticket.py` — drift assertion (packaged guide quotes
  `EXPECTED_FORMAT`).
- `tests/unit/test_plan_review_explain.py` — `rebar explain commit-trailer` returns the format;
  unknown-guide error lists it; LINK-RESOLUTION guard test (below).
- `AGENTS.md` — one-line pointer to `rebar explain commit-trailer` in the trailer bullet.
- `docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` — note the CLI discovery paths.

## Testing

- Happy: `registry.explain_guide("commit-trailer")` and CLI `rebar explain commit-trailer` both
  return text containing `EXPECTED_FORMAT` (exit 0).
- Edge: unknown guide name raises `ExplainError("unknown-id", …)` whose message now lists
  `commit-trailer`.
- Drift: the packaged guide contains `EXPECTED_FORMAT` verbatim.
- Link-resolution guard (the core regression-proofing): a single test enumerates ALL
  in-command help prose a repo-less client can see — every registered `AUTHOR_GUIDES` guide
  body AND every `explain_criterion(<id>)` rendering — extracts every markdown link `](target)`,
  and asserts each target RESOLVES for a non-rebar client: allowed iff `https://`/`http://`
  (off-repo), an intra-doc `#anchor`, or `mailto:`. Any consumer-relative path (`](*.md)`,
  `](docs/…)`, `](../…)`) FAILS. It also validates every `rebar explain <topic>` mentioned in
  that prose names a real registered guide/criterion, so the reachable-pointer claim can't rot.
  Driven by the registries (not a hardcoded file list) so new guides/criteria are covered
  automatically.
- Full `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar explain commit-trailer` exits 0 and prints the canonical trailer format (run it;
      CLI unit test asserts `EXPECTED_FORMAT` in stdout).
- [ ] `commit-trailer` is listed among known guides in `rebar explain` help and the
      `unknown-id` error (unit test).
- [ ] The packaged `commit-ticket-trailer.md` quotes `EXPECTED_FORMAT` verbatim (drift test).
- [ ] The link-resolution guard passes: across every `AUTHOR_GUIDES` guide body and every
      `explain_criterion(<id>)` rendering, no markdown link target is consumer-relative (only
      `https://`/`#anchor`/`mailto:`), and every `rebar explain <topic>` cited names a real
      registered guide/criterion (test passes; verified by grep too).
- [ ] `rebar explain review` and `rebar explain plan` no longer reference repo-relative files;
      sibling guides read as `rebar explain <topic>`, repo docs as `https://github.com/navapbc/…`
      (verified by reading the rendered guides).
- [ ] AGENTS.md's trailer bullet names `rebar explain commit-trailer` (grep).
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, `make test` all pass (run them).
data.title: Make commit-trailer format + CLI-served guides resolve for repo-less clients
